So the thing I'm continually seeing across blog posts is a over focus on local payment methods.
And the reason this exists is because it is pervasive throughout our content.
So that's that's on us.
But we've had a pretty big pivot in the last year.
And what's happened is we found that our best market is Europe and the UK.
And in Europe and the UK, we don't really offer very many local payment methods.
We offer credit cards.
So whereas like in all these other markets in Asia, we went nuts.
And like integrated all these payment methods.
And we don't do that much business in Asia.
In LATAM right now, we're not doing that much.
In Europe, where we are doing a lot of business, we don't really offer local payment methods.
So we've really shifted our focus from, hey, you've got to localize your payment methods to, hey, Europe, you need to work with a direct card acquirer.
And that's coming through a lot of the copy.
And some of the things that are like focuses that we've completely abandoned are in there.
So I see India and China come up a lot as examples, both of which are great examples of places where local payment methods are important.
But we pulled out of both of those markets.
We don't work there at all.
So, you know, seeing examples on like use UPI for payments in India, it's just not relevant to us because we don't support UPI anymore.
We don't support India anymore.
Same thing with China.
So I think we need to.
Dial down the focus on local payment methods and payment localization, and where we're talking about that in articles, replace it with talking about local card acquiring.
And local card acquiring means that your card acquiring process, or the one who's processing your transactions, is in the same market as the customer who's making the purchase.
And the processor processing of those transactions, the acquirer, is in the same market.
You get lower fraud rates because they understand that market and their fraud trends better.
You get lower or higher approval rates because, again, you understand that market better.
You're able to approve more transactions.
It's just a big benefit for everybody.
There's no middleman that you're going through.
So that's the message that we need to get in here.
can give you some specifics about that.
